subject: Project Management
title: The Supermarket Inspiration
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Supermarket Inspiration.

hook.text: Ohno got his biggest idea not from a factory, but from an American supermarket.

buildup.text: In supermarkets, shelves are restocked only when customers take items. Supply follows demand.

discovery.text: Ohno applied this to Toyota: each station pulled parts only when the next station needed them.

twist.text: This 'pull system' eliminated warehouses of unused parts. Inventory costs plummeted.

climax.text: A grocery store visit turned into the foundation of just-in-time manufacturing.

punchline.text: The factory of the future was inspired by a grocery aisle.

quiz.question: What did Ohno learn from American supermarkets?
options: Supply should follow demand — the pull system
Build large inventories to prepare for spikes
